# Client Qualification Scorecard

> The Meritage 100-point qualification scoring instrument. Complete this scorecard after the discovery conversation. The score informs the qualification decision — it does not replace advisor judgment.
>
> **Template type:** Scoring instrument
> **Used in:** Module 02 (Qualification)
> **Paired with:** qualification-discovery-questionnaire.md
>
> ---
>
> ## Instructions
>
> Complete this scorecard within 48 hours of the discovery conversation, while observations are fresh. Score each criterion based on what was actually observed or documented — not on what the prospect claimed or what you hope will be true.
>
> Each category has a maximum score and a set of criteria. Assign a score to each criterion using the scoring guidance provided. The category score is the sum of its criteria scores. The total score is the sum of all category scores (maximum: 100 points).
>
> Complete the scorecard before communicating the qualification decision to the prospect.
>
> ---
>
> ## Prospect Information
>
> **Business name:** ___________________________________
> **Industry:** ___________________________________
> **Approximate revenue (TTM):** ___________________________________
> **Approximate EBITDA (TTM):** ___________________________________
> **Owner name:** ___________________________________
> **Referral source:** ___________________________________
> **Discovery conversation date:** ___________________________________
> **Scorecard completed by:** ___________________________________
> **Scorecard completion date:** ___________________________________
>
> ---
>
> ## Category 1: Financial Quality (20 points maximum)
>
> *Does the business have financial performance that is defensible, documentable, and normalizable?*
>
> ### 1.1 Financial Statement Quality (0–6 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 6 | CPA-prepared accrual-basis financials for 3+ years; clean and well-organized |
> | 4–5 | CPA-prepared financials but cash-basis, or minor gaps in the record |
> | 2–3 | Internally prepared financials; organized but not CPA-reviewed |
> | 0–1 | Financials are missing, disorganized, or have material irregularities |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 6
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> ### 1.2 EBITDA Defensibility (0–8 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 7–8 | Normalized EBITDA is clearly documentable; addbacks are reasonable and documented |
> | 5–6 | Addbacks are identifiable but require documentation; no obvious red flags |
> | 3–4 | Significant addbacks that are not yet documented; some risk of buyer challenge |
> | 0–2 | Addbacks are aggressive, undocumented, or based solely on owner assertion |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 8
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> ### 1.3 Revenue Trend (0–6 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 5–6 | Revenue growing consistently over 3 years; growth is organic and explainable |
> | 3–4 | Revenue stable or modest growth; any decline is explainable with a recovery trajectory |
> | 1–2 | Revenue declining or highly variable; no clear recovery narrative |
> | 0 | Revenue declining materially with no credible explanation |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 6
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> **Category 1 Total:** ___ / 20
>
> ---
>
> ## Category 2: Seller Readiness (20 points maximum)
>
> *Is the Seller genuinely motivated, realistic, and a productive advisor relationship?*
>
> ### 2.1 Seller Motivation (0–8 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 7–8 | Seller has a clear, genuine reason to transact; motivated and realistic about timing |
> | 5–6 | Seller is motivated but has some flexibility on timeline or is still weighing options |
> | 3–4 | Seller is exploring the market without a firm commitment to transact |
> | 0–2 | Seller motivation is unclear, or the driver is a distress event that creates timeline pressure |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 8
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> ### 2.2 Valuation Expectations Alignment (0–8 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 7–8 | Seller's expectations are aligned with or below market; engages constructively with data |
> | 5–6 | Seller's expectations are slightly above market (within 20%); appears willing to engage with data |
> | 3–4 | Seller's expectations are materially above market (20–40%); has not yet engaged with comparable data |
> | 0–2 | Seller's expectations are more than 40% above market, or Seller rejected market data when presented |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 8
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> ### 2.3 Advisor Receptivity (0–4 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 4 | Seller listens actively, asks good questions, and demonstrates openness to advice |
> | 2–3 | Seller is generally receptive but has fixed views on some aspects of the process |
> | 0–1 | Seller is defensive, dismissive of concerns, or unwilling to consider advisor input |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 4
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> **Category 2 Total:** ___ / 20
>
> ---
>
> ## Category 3: Operational Maturity (20 points maximum)
>
> *Can this business operate and generate value without the current owner?*
>
> ### 3.1 Management Team Depth (0–8 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 7–8 | Experienced management team in place; can operate the business independently; retention likely |
> | 5–6 | Partial management team; some owner dependence but key functions are covered |
> | 3–4 | Limited management below the owner; significant coaching and transition planning needed |
> | 0–2 | No meaningful management team; business is fully owner-dependent |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 8
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> ### 3.2 Owner Independence (0–8 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 7–8 | Owner is not essential to daily operations; business can run without them |
> | 5–6 | Owner is involved but not irreplaceable; transition planning is feasible |
> | 3–4 | Owner is central to several key functions; transition will require significant planning |
> | 0–2 | Owner is involved in all key customer relationships and operational decisions; no succession path |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 8
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> ### 3.3 Process and Documentation (0–4 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 4 | Key processes are documented; SOPs exist; business is explainable to a new owner |
> | 2–3 | Some documentation exists; core processes are understandable even if not fully written |
> | 0–1 | Operations are largely undocumented; knowledge lives in the owner's head |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 4
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> **Category 3 Total:** ___ / 20
>
> ---
>
> ## Category 4: Market Attractiveness (15 points maximum)
>
> *Is there an active, interested buyer market for this type of business?*
>
> ### 4.1 Sector M&A Activity (0–6 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 5–6 | Active M&A market in this sector; multiple recent comparable transactions; multiple buyer categories |
> | 3–4 | Moderate M&A activity; buyer market exists but is not highly competitive |
> | 1–2 | Limited M&A activity; buyer universe is narrow or inactive |
> | 0 | No meaningful M&A activity in this sector; no identified buyer categories |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 6
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> ### 4.2 Revenue Concentration Risk (0–5 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 5 | No single customer represents more than 15% of revenue |
> | 3–4 | Top customer represents 15–25% of revenue; risk is manageable |
> | 1–2 | Top customer represents 25–40% of revenue; will require mitigation narrative |
> | 0 | Top customer represents more than 40% of revenue; material concentration risk |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 5
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> ### 4.3 Competitive Position (0–4 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 4 | Business has identifiable, durable competitive advantages that will survive ownership transition |
> | 2–3 | Business has some competitive differentiation; advantages are partially tied to the owner |
> | 0–1 | Business competes primarily on price or relationships that will not transfer |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 4
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> **Category 4 Total:** ___ / 15
>
> ---
>
> ## Category 5: Growth Potential (15 points maximum)
>
> *Can a buyer create value beyond the current financial performance?*
>
> ### 5.1 Organic Growth Levers (0–6 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 5–6 | Multiple identifiable growth avenues (geographic, product, customer) not yet pursued |
> | 3–4 | Some growth potential exists; one or two levers are identifiable |
> | 1–2 | Growth potential is limited; business appears fully optimized |
> | 0 | Market is contracting; no growth narrative is plausible |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 6
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> ### 5.2 Platform or Strategic Value (0–5 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 5 | Business is a clear platform for consolidation, or has high strategic value to a specific buyer category |
> | 3–4 | Some platform or strategic potential exists for a financial or strategic buyer |
> | 1–2 | Limited strategic value; business is likely to trade primarily on financial metrics |
> | 0 | No identified strategic value; business will be valued as a standalone financial asset only |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 5
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> ### 5.3 Management Capacity for Growth (0–4 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 4 | Management team has the capacity and appetite to execute a growth plan |
> | 2–3 | Growth potential exists but would require leadership investment |
> | 0–1 | No management capacity for growth; business requires stabilization first |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 4
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> **Category 5 Total:** ___ / 15
>
> ---
>
> ## Category 6: Transaction Readiness (10 points maximum)
>
> *Is the business prepared for the documentation and process demands of a sale?*
>
> ### 6.1 Financial Documentation Readiness (0–4 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 4 | Three years of CPA financials, tax returns, and addback documentation available now |
> | 2–3 | Most documentation available; minor gaps that can be addressed quickly |
> | 0–1 | Significant documentation gaps that will delay the engagement or create diligence risk |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 4
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> ### 6.2 Legal and Corporate Documentation (0–3 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 3 | Customer contracts, vendor agreements, corporate records, and IP documentation are organized |
> | 1–2 | Some documentation gaps; no major legal issues identified |
> | 0 | Significant documentation gaps or pending legal/regulatory issues |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 3
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> ### 6.3 Professional Advisor Readiness (0–3 points)
>
> | Score | Description |
> |---|---|
> | 3 | Transaction-experienced CPA and attorney are engaged or immediately available |
> | 1–2 | CPA and attorney exist but may not have transaction experience; will need guidance |
> | 0 | No CPA or attorney engaged; Seller is resistant to engaging professional advisors |
>
> **Score assigned:** ___ / 3
> **Notes:** _______________________________________________
>
> **Category 6 Total:** ___ / 10
>
> ---
>
> ## Score Summary
>
> | Category | Maximum | Score |
> |---|---|---|
> | 1. Financial Quality | 20 | ___ |
> | 2. Seller Readiness | 20 | ___ |
> | 3. Operational Maturity | 20 | ___ |
> | 4. Market Attractiveness | 15 | ___ |
> | 5. Growth Potential | 15 | ___ |
> | 6. Transaction Readiness | 10 | ___ |
> | **Total** | **100** | **___** |
>
> ---
>
> ## Score Interpretation
>
> | Score | Interpretation | Default Action |
> |---|---|---|
> | 80–100 | Strong candidate | Proceed to engagement |
> | 65–79 | Qualified with identified risks | Proceed; document and monitor risks |
> | 50–64 | Conditional | Define specific conditions; reassess before engaging |
> | Below 50 | Not qualified at this time | Decline; document reason; offer referral |
>
> **Note:** The score interpretation is a starting point, not a final decision. An advisor may override the default action in either direction based on factors not captured in the score — with documented rationale.
>
> ---
>
> ## Disqualifying Findings
>
> *Check any that apply. A checked item produces a decline recommendation regardless of total score, unless a specific remediation path is documented.*
>
> - [ ] Seller motivation is not genuine (exploring only, no intent to transact)
> - [ ] - [ ] Valuation expectations rejected market data; gap exceeds 40%
> - [ ] - [ ] Financial statements cannot be normalized; material irregularity identified
> - [ ] - [ ] Owner dependence is complete; no succession path feasible before market
> - [ ] - [ ] Material undisclosed issue identified (litigation, regulatory, environmental)
> - [ ] - [ ] Advisor-Seller relationship concern is sufficient to indicate an adversarial engagement
> - [ ] - [ ] Timeline pressure creates structural disadvantage to the Seller
>
> - [ ] **Disqualifying finding present:** Yes / No
>
> - [ ] If yes — remediation path: _______________________________________________
>
> - [ ] ---
